note:
据英国《金融时报》报道，维珍Hyperloop最近解雇了一半员工，因为该公司正将重心从客运转向货运。据员工称，该公司共有111名员工在意外情况下被终止服务。
这家总部位于洛杉矶的公司最初成立于2014年，此前埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）发表了一份白皮书，阐述了用真空管远距离运送人员的潜力。该公司参加了马斯克资助的hyperloop竞赛，并于2017年在理查德·布兰森（Richard Branson）领导的维珍集团（Virgin Group）中找到了一位著名投资者。虽然该公司已经成功地推进了概念样机，甚至在2020年底完成了一项针对人类乘客的试验，但这个想法还没有实现&#39；我真的找不到很多人。
公司发言人告诉2019冠状病毒疾病已经改变了很多事情，公司正转移注意力以满足全球供应链的需求。凭借精简的员工结构，该公司有望在专注于货运领域的同时变得更加灵活、成本效益更高。
持有维珍Hyperloop 76%股份的国有物流集团DP World告诉英国《金融时报》，其15个潜在客户对通过维珍Hyperloop的基础设施而非人工运输货物感兴趣，因为这涉及的风险较小，需要的监管批准也较少。
然而，根据《财富》杂志的一份报告，该公司在其有生之年只找到了一个潜在客户，即沙特阿拉伯，该国目前热衷于在其港口城市吉达和首都利雅得之间建设一条超环线货运通道。DP World发言人还告诉英国《金融时报》，该项目的利润可以在未来再投资于建造与人类兼容的Virgin Hyperloop。
根据2016年泄露的文件，建造超级环路系统的成本是马斯克最初在白皮书中预期的数倍。根据施工地点的不同，有许多变量需要考虑。
每英里Hyperloop的估计成本在阿联酋为5200万美元，在Hyperloop为1.21亿美元#39；它的家乡是加利福尼亚州。这些远不止麝香&#39；该公司的预测是每英里1100万美元。不要忘记确保管道真空的持续维护成本，没有真空，列车无法达到计划速度。
这可能是马斯克本人不愿在这个概念上投入时间的主要原因之一。他那无聊的公司转而使用汽车在一个隧道式网络中接送乘客，这个网络在拉斯维加斯已经开始发展。
凭借其最近的转向，维珍Hyperloop加入了总部位于荷兰的Hardt Hyperloop。两年多来，Hardt Hyperloop一直专注于将其Hyperloop基础设施用于货物运输。
note_en:
Virgin Hyperloop recently fired half of its workforce as the company is shifting its focus from transporting passengers to cargo,   Financial Times reported. Services of a total of 111 members of its staff were terminated in what was an unanticipated move according to the employees.
 The Los Angeles-based company was originally founded in 2014 after Elon Musk shared a white paper about the potential of transporting people across long distances in vacuum tubes. The company participated in Musk-funded hyperloop competitions and found a famous investor in Richard Branson-led Virgin Group in 2017. While the company has managed to advance the concept prototype and even completed  a trial with human passengers in late 2020, the idea hasn't really found many takers.
  A company spokesperson told FT that the COVID-19 pandemic had changed many things and the company was shifting focus to address the needs of the global supply chain. With a leaner staff setup, the company was hopeful of becoming more agile and cost-efficient as it focused on the cargo segment.
  DP World, a state-owned logistics group that holds a 76 percent stake in Virgin Hyperloop, told FT that 15 of its potential customers were interested in moving cargo through its infrastructure rather than humans since it involved less risk and required less regulatory approvals.
 According to a   Fortune report though, the company has found only one potential client in its lifetime, Saudi Arabia which is now keen on building a hyperloop cargo link between its port city of Jeddah and capital city of Riyadh. DP World spokesperson also told FT that profits from the project could be reinvested in building the human-compatible Virgin Hyperloop in the future.
  According to documents that were leaked in 2016, the  cost of building Hyperloop systems was many times over what Musk had originally anticipated in his whitepaper. Depending on the location of the construction, there are many variables that need to be taken into account.
  Estimated costs for a mile of Hyperloop have varied between $52 million dollars in the UAE to $121 million in Hyperloop's home state of California. These are way beyond Musk's projections of $11 million a mile. Not to forget the ongoing maintenance costs of ensuring vacuum in the tubes without which the trains cannot achieve their planned speeds.
 This is probably one of the main reasons why Musk, himself has stayed away from investing his own time in the concept. His Boring Company is instead using cars to shuttle people in a tunneled network that has  begun to grow in Vegas.
 With its recent pivot, Virgin Hyperloop joined Netherlands-based Hardt Hyperloop that has been focusing on using its hyperloop infrastructure for cargo transport for over two years.
title: 维珍Hyperloop将重心从乘客转移到了货物
title_en: Virgin Hyperloop Shifts Its Focus From Passengers To Cargo

note:
利用量子技术找到了一个隐藏在地下的物体——这是一个期待已久的里程碑，对工业、人类知识和国家安全具有深远影响。英国伯明翰大学量子技术中心在传感器和计时方面的研究人员报告了他们在自然界的成就。这是世界上第一台非实验室条件下的量子重力梯度仪。
量子重力梯度仪是根据国防部的合同开发的，也是在UKRI资助的重力先锋项目中开发的，用于在现实世界条件下发现埋在地下一米以下的室外隧道。它赢得了一场将技术带到国外的国际竞赛。
该传感器的工作原理是利用量子物理原理检测微重力的变化，量子物理原理基于在亚分子水平上操纵自然。
这一成功开辟了一条商业道路，大大改善了对地下存在物的测绘。
伯明翰大学冷原子物理系负责人Kai Bongs和英国量子技术中心传感器和计时的首席研究员说：这是一个&#39；爱迪生时刻&#39；这将改变社会、人类理解和经济。
"有了这一突破，我们有可能在探索、建造和修复过程中结束对糟糕记录和运气的依赖。此外，一张目前看不见的地下地图现在离我们又近了一大步，结束了我们对南极洲的了解比街道下面几英尺深的地方更多的局面"
目前的重力传感器受到一系列环境因素的限制。一个特别的挑战是振动，这限制了测量应用中所有重力传感器的测量时间。如果能够解决这些限制，调查可以变得更快、更全面、成本更低。
该传感器由伯明翰原子干涉仪负责人、该研究的主要作者迈克尔·霍林斯基博士及其伯明翰团队开发，是一种重力梯度仪。他们的系统克服了振动和其他各种环境挑战，以便成功地应用于该领域。
这项由工程学院的Nicole Metje教授领导的成功检测是一项长期发展计划的最终成果，该计划从一开始就与最终用户密切相关。
这一突破将使未来的重力测量变得更便宜、更可靠，交付速度提高10倍，将测量所需的时间从一个月减少到几天。它有可能为勘测打开一系列新的应用程序，为地下提供一个新的镜头。
RSK地球科学与工程总监乔治·塔克韦尔教授说：&#34；探测地面条件，如矿井、隧道和不稳定地面，对我们设计、建造和维护住房、工业和基础设施的能力至关重要。这项新技术所代表的改进能力可能会改变我们规划和交付这些项目的方式"
这项突破是伯明翰大学、环境、工程和可持续解决方案提供商RSK、Dstl（国防科学技术实验室、英国国防部的一部分）和技术公司TeleDeNe E2V的合作。
note_en:
An object hidden below ground has been located using quantum technology—a long-awaited milestone with profound implications for industry, human knowledge and national security.    University of Birmingham researchers from the UK National Quantum Technology Hub in Sensors and Timing have reported their achievement in  Nature. It is the first in the world for a quantum gravity gradiometer outside of laboratory conditions.
  The quantum gravity gradiometer, which was developed under a contract for the Ministry of Defense and in the UKRI-funded Gravity Pioneer project, was used to find a tunnel buried outdoors in real-world conditions one meter below the ground surface. It wins an international race to take the technology outside.
  The sensor works by detecting variations in microgravity using the principles of quantum physics, which is based on manipulating nature at the sub-molecular level.
  The success opens a commercial path to significantly improved mapping of what exists below ground level.
      Professor Kai Bongs, head of cold atom physics at the University of Birmingham and principal investigator of the UK Quantum Technology Hub Sensors and Timing, said: "This is an 'Edison moment' in sensing that will transform society, human understanding and economies.
  "With this breakthrough we have the potential to end reliance on poor records and luck as we explore, build and repair. In addition, an underground map of what is currently invisible is now a significant step closer, ending a situation where we know more about Antarctica than what lies a few feet below our streets."
  Current gravity sensors are limited by a range of environmental factors. A particular challenge is vibration, which limits the measurement time of all gravity sensors for survey applications. If these limitations can be addressed, surveys can become faster, more comprehensive and lower cost.
  The sensor developed by Dr. Michael Holynski, Head of Atom Interferometry at Birmingham and lead author of the study, and his team at Birmingham is a gravity gradiometer. Their system overcomes vibration and a variety of other environmental challenges in order to successfully apply   in the field.
  The successful detection, realized in collaboration with   led by Professor Nicole Metje of the School of Engineering, is the culmination of a long-term development program that has been closely linked to end-users from its outset.
  This breakthrough will allow future gravity surveys to be cheaper, more reliable and delivered 10 times faster, reducing the time needed for surveys from a month to a few days. It has the potential to open a range of new applications for   survey, providing a new lens into the underground.
  Professor George Tuckwell, director for geoscience and engineering at RSK, said: "Detection of ground conditions such as mine workings, tunnels and unstable ground is fundamental to our ability to design, construct and maintain housing, industry and infrastructure. The improved capability that this new technology represents could transform how we map the ground and deliver these projects."
  The breakthrough is a collaboration between the University of Birmingham, environmental, engineering and sustainability solutions provider RSK, Dstl (the Defense Science and Technology Laboratory, part of the UK Ministry of Defense), and technology company Teledyne e2v.
title: 传感器的突破为绘制突破性的地球表面下世界地图铺平了道路

你能指出马克思说过或写过的任何支持你所说的&#34；马克思主义式社会主义&#34；？
"... 只有一种方法可以缩短、简化和集中旧社会的血腥死亡痛苦和新社会的血腥分娩痛苦，那就是革命恐怖" -- 卡尔·马克思，《维也纳反革命的胜利》，[marxists.org]Neue Rheinische Zeitung第136期，1848年11月。
"下一次世界大战不仅会导致反动阶级和反动王朝的消失，还会导致整个反动民族的消失。这也是向前迈出的一步" -- 弗里德里希·恩格斯，《马加亚的斗争》，[anu.edu.au]Neue Rheinsche Zeitung第194号，1849年1月。
问题是，在马克思主义伦理学中#39；这是一个核心道德价值观，它是第一位的、最重要的和中心的，其他一切都是次要的。它&#39；这是使世界失去联系的责任。基本的观点是，我们认为对错是物质生产方式的结果，也就是说，没有绝对的正确或错误，他们和39。你总是依赖其他东西。因此，以一种无法预测的方式，改变这一点会改变什么是对的，什么是错的。因此，革命者不应该&#39；I don’我不太在乎今天被视为对与错的东西，重要的是要改变某些东西，无论付出什么代价，因为最终这将创造新的对与错，它们&#39；我们将感谢子孙后代帮助我们带来了正确的（新的）东西——不管是什么。
那&#39；这就是为什么，从马克思主义的角度来看，屠杀人民不是&#39；这不一定是错的。它&#39；严格地说，如果而且只有当这样的屠杀会阻碍世界的分裂，那就错了。它&#39；如果它是&#39；我将帮助实现这种不认同。至于其他情况，比如大屠杀既不会阻碍也不会帮助分裂，它&#39；s建议马克思主义者遵循当前的道德观，因为这有助于在接下来的时间里，当他们需要做一些可能与之相反的事情时，获得民众的支持——例如，需要引起饥荒。
note_en:
can you point to anything said or written by Marx that would have supported what you call "Marxist style socialism"?
  "... there is only one way in which the murderous death agonies of the old society and the bloody birth throes of the new society can be shortened, simplified and concentrated, and that way is revolutionary terror." -- Karl Marx,  The Victory of the Counter-Revolution in Vienna, [marxists.org] Neue Rheinische Zeitung No. 136, Nov. 1848.
  "The next world war will result in the disappearance from the face of the earth not only of reactionary classes and dynasties, but also of entire reactionary peoples. And that, too, is a step forward." -- Friedrich Engels,  The Magyar Struggle, [anu.edu.au] Neue Rheinische Zeitung No. 194, Jan. 1849.
 The thing is, in Marxist ethics there's one core moral value that comes first, front and center, everything else being secondary to it. It's the duty to disalienate the world. The fundamental idea is that what we consider right or wrong is a result of the material mode of production, that is, there is no absolute right or wrong, they're always dependent of something else. Changing this something else therefore changes what counts as right and what counts as wrong, in a way that cannot be predicted. Therefore, the revolutionary shouldn't care much about what today is seen as right and wrong, what counts is changing that something, whatever it takes, as in the end this will create new rights and wrongs, and they'll be thanked by future generations for having helped bring the (new) right things -- whatever those may be.
 That's why, from a Marxist perspective, massacring people isn't necessarily wrong. It's wrong, strictly speaking, if, and only if, doing such a massacre would hinder the disalienation of the world. And it's strictly right if it'll help bring about that disalienation. As for other circumstances, such as when committing a massacre will neither hinder nor help disalienation, it's recommended the Marxist follow current morality, as that helps gather popular support for when, down the line, they need do something that might go against it -- such as the need to cause a famine, for example.
